roles.core: central bot, D1 authority, schema migration, shared Marzban configuration, ownership ledger, update controller and financial reconciliation
roles.edge: reseller bots, customer sales/service UI, shared Marzban service lifecycle and reseller management
roles.processor: scheduled shared Marzban usage polling, automatic debits, service health, backups and background automation

features: shared Marzban settings are visible directly in the main central web dashboard
central bot has a dedicated Marzban management screen with URL, username, password, price, batch, proxies, inbounds, debt policy and connection test
central web dashboard can save, test, enable and disable the shared Marzban backend
password messages sent to the central bot are deleted after storage
resellers never receive Marzban administrator credentials or tokens
all shared Marzban ownership and per-reseller usage billing features from 3.3.21 remain included

deployment.automatic_provisioning: upload the ZIP through the central bot; deployment remains pinned to the exact GitHub commit produced by the updater
deployment.staged_compatibility: Core, Edge and Processor self-create shared tables/indexes and add missing trial-usage columns during staged deployment
deployment.credential_scope: configure Marzban from central dashboard Settings or central bot Management -> Marzban Shared; credentials remain central-only
deployment.billing_scope: one account per reseller bot, one ownership record per panel username, byte-delta accounting with integer remainder preservation
deployment.debt_policy: optional automatic reseller suspension when usage debt remains after wallet collection
deployment.master_margin: child reseller price can include the configured upstream markup; collected margin is credited to the parent wallet once per event

validation.direct_pasarguard_service_call_scan: passed; service lifecycle uses provider dispatcher
validation.live_marzban_connection: not run without production panel credentials
validation.live_cloudflare_deployment: not run without production Cloudflare credentials
validation.central_web_marzban_form: passed; visible form in main /control settings page
validation.central_bot_marzban_menu: passed; direct management button and complete settings workflow
validation.central_web_action_handlers: passed; save and test handlers present with HTTPS and JSON validation

limitations: Marzban Proxies and Inbounds JSON must match the configuration available on the connected Marzban installation
services created directly in Marzban outside the bot are not billed to a reseller until they are imported or assigned through the bot
production API compatibility depends on the Marzban version and permissions of the configured shared admin account
